## GATE Previous Year Question Papers Subject Wise PDF

The Previous Year GATE Question Paper include 65 multiple-choice questions (MCQs), multiple-select questions (MSQs), and numerical answer type (NAT) questions. Each question is worth 1 or 2 marks.

According to the GATE Exam Pattern, the General Aptitude section accounts for 15% of the total marks, the Engineering Mathematics section covers 13%, and the core subject paper accounts for the remaining 72%.

The GATE question paper for subjects such as CY, AR, DA, EY, GG, MA, PH, XH, ST, and XL, on the other hand, consists of only two sections: general aptitude worth 15 percent and core subject paper worth 85 percent. [...] |  |  |

| Organizing Institute | IIT Guwahati |
| Exam Mode | Online |

| Category | Entrance Exam |
| Exam Duration | 3 hours |
| Total Marks | 100 |
| Number of Questions | 65 |
| Types of Questions |  Multiple-Choice Questions (MCQs)  Multiple-Select Questions  Numerical Answer Type (NAT) Questions |
| Marking Scheme |  1 or 2 marks per question  1/3 negative marking for 1-mark MCQs  2/3 negative marking for 2-mark MCQs |
| Sections |  General Aptitude  Engineering Mathematics  Concerned Discipline |
| GATE Official Website 2026 | gate2026.iitg.ac.in |

## Steps to Download the GATE Previous Years Question Paper PDF [...] Aspirants who wish to download the subject-wise GATE previous year question papers with solutions book PDFfrom the IIT Roorkee's official website can follow the steps outlined below:

Step 1:  Visit the GATE exam conducting authority's official website, i.e., gate.iitr.ac.in.

Step 2:  Look out for the "Download" option available in the middle of the landing page.

Step 3:  To download the GATE previous year's question papers, select the year from 2019 to 2025 as well as the desired subject.

Step 4:  Click on the preferred subject option from the 30 subject lists provided on the screen.

Step 5:  After clicking, the GATE Previous Year Question Paper PDF will be ready to view or download.

## GATE Previous Year Question Papers Subject Wise PDF

query: "GATE" "Profit and Loss" previous year question with solution MCQ numerical PDF OR gateoverflow OR official paper
title: Profit and Loss: Aptitude Questions & Ans - Free Practice!
content:
###### Q. 5 Two entrepreneurs, A and B, pool their resources to start a venture. A invests ₹40,000, while B invests ₹50,000. They agree to split profits in proportion to their investments. If their business earns a total profit of ₹13,500 in the first year, how much of this profit does B take home?

Ans: ₹7,500

Investment ratio of A and B = 40,000 : 50,000 = 4 : 5.  
B’s share = 5/(4+5) × 13,500 = 5/9 × 13,500 = ₹7,500.

###### Q. 6 How much additional money you will save tentatively if you bought given shoe for 60% discount?

New Selling Price = 15000 \ (1-0.6) = 6000

Original Selling Price = 9000

Additional Discount = 9000 – 6000 = 3000 [...] ###### Q. 2 Two friends, A and B, partner up to start a business. A invests ₹50,000, and B contributes ₹40,000. After a few months, B notices he’s entitled to a profit share of ₹6,000. Curious about how their business is doing, they ask their accountant: what is the total profit generated by their venture?

Ans: C) ₹13,500

The profit ratio of A and B = 50,000 : 40,000 = 5 : 4.  
Let total profit be P. Then, B’s share = 4/(5+4) × P = 4/9 × P.  
Given, 4/9 × P = 6,000.  
P = 6,000 × 9 / 4 = ₹13,500.

###### Q. 3 A merchant known for his clever pricing strategy mixes two types of sugar: one costing ₹24 per kg and the other ₹30 per kg. He blends them in the ratio of 1:2 and sets a selling price that gives him a 20% profit. Can you determine the selling price of his mixture per kg? [...] So the answer is -7

###### Q. 14 A clothing store owner purchased two equal-sized batches of dresses at identical costs. The owner sold every dress from the first batch for 70 each, making a profit of 1,600. The second batch was sold for 90 per dress, bringing in a profit of 2,400. How many dresses were there in total in both batches?

Ans: D

Lets say there were x number of dresses in each shipment. And cost of the each dress is y. Then we can set up following equation:

70x – xy = 1600

90x -xy = 2400

20x = 800

x= 40

Therefore there are 80 dresses in both shipments

###### Q. 15 A shopkeeper experiences a 20% loss on a defective mobile phone when compared to its selling price. If the phone originally cost 6000, what is the selling price?

Suppose selling price is x; Loss = 0.2x

### 5. Focus on Scoring Sections

Some parts of the paper are easier to score if you practice them regularly. General Aptitude and Logical Reasoning usually do not need heavy memory but require practice. Similarly, Mathematics is common for all and can give good marks if revised well.

 Practice General Aptitude questions daily.
 Revise Mathematics formulas regularly.
 Use GATE pyq for aptitude and maths to see question trends.
 Do not ignore small scoring topics, as they add to your total.

query: "SSC" "Averages" previous year question with solution MCQ numerical PDF OR gateoverflow OR official paper
title: Previous Year Average Questions For SSC CGL Mains/Tier II till date
content:
Q10. The average weight of A, B and C is 45 kg. If the average weight of A and B be 40 kg and that of B and C be 43 kg, then the weight of B is  
(a) 31  
(b) 17  
(c) 20  
(d) 26  
{Held on: Apr 12, 2015}

Ans: (a) 31

Explanation:

A + B + C = 135 \_\_\_\_\_(1)  
A + B = 80 \_\_\_\_\_(2)  
B + C = 86 \_\_\_\_\_(3)

(2) + (3) – (1) ⟶  
B = 166 – 135  
    = 31

Q11. In a team of 10 persons, nine persons spent ₹40 each for their meal and the remaining one spent ₹9 more than the average expenditure of all the 10 persons. The total expenditure for their meal was  
(a) 410  
(b) 610  
(c) 510  
(d) 310  
{Held on: Apr 12, 2015}

Ans: a) 410

Explanation:

Let, avg expenditure of 10 person = x ⇒ Sum = 10x  
n = 9, avg = 40 ⇒ sum = 360  
Expenditure of 10th person = x + 9 [...] 11 part = 3 × 110  
6 part = 180

Q11. The average daily income of 7 men, 11 women and 2 boys is Rs. 257.50. If the average daily income of the men is Rs. 10 more than that of women and the average daily income of the women is Rs. 10 more than that of boys, the average daily income of a man is  
(a) ₹277.5  
(b) ₹250  
(c) ₹265  
(d) ₹257  
{Held on: Dec 1, 2016}

Ans: (c) ₹265

Explanation:

7(x + 20) + 11(x + 10) + 2x = 20 × 257.50   
⇒ 7x + 140 + 11x + 110 + 2x = 20 × 257.5  
⇒ 20x + 250 = 20 × 257.5  
⇒ 2x + 25 = 515 [By dividing both side by 10]  
⇒ 2x = 490  
⇒ x = 245

∴ x + 20 = 265 [...] 100 mod 13 = 9  
First No = 100 – 9 + 13 = 104

By mod we have to find remainder. In this case we need to find remainder when 100 is divided by 13

200 mod 13 = 5  
Last No = 200 – 5 = 195

\begin{align\} \text{Avg} &=\frac{\text{first term}+\text{last term}}{2}\\ &= \frac{104+195}{2}\\ &= \frac{299}{2}\\ &= 149.5\\ \end{align\}

Q15. The average weight of L, M and N is 93 kg. If the average weight of L and M be 89 kg and that of M and N be 96.5 kg, then the weight (in kg) of M is \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_.  
(a) 92  
(b) 86  
(c) 101  
(d) 95  
{Held on: Feb 21, 2018}

Ans: (a) 92

Explanation:

L + M + N = 279 \_\_\_\_\_(1)  
L + M = 178 \_\_\_\_\_(2)  
M + N = 193 \_\_\_\_\_(3)

(2) + (3) – (1) ⟶  
M = 371 – 279  
    = 92

query: "SSC" "Profit and Loss" previous year question with solution MCQ numerical PDF OR gateoverflow OR official paper
title: SSC Profit and Loss Questions Solved Problems with Detailed ...
content:
Previous Year Papers [...] Test Series [...] #### SSC Profit and Loss Questions Question 7:

A shopkeeper increases the price of an object by 40% and then sells it at a 25% discount on the marked price. If the selling price of such an object be Rs. 2100, its cost price for the shopkeeper was?

1. 3000
2. 1500
3. 1750
4. 2000

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

Option 4 : 2000

#### SSC Profit and Loss Questions Question 7 Detailed Solution

25% = 1/4 and 40% = 2/5

SP of the article = Rs. 2100

MP of the book = 2100 × (4/3)

∴ CP of the book = 2100 × (4/3) × (5/7) = Rs. 2000

A alone can do a piece of work in 6 days, and B alone in 8 days. A and B undertook to do it for Rs. 3200. With the help of C, they finished in 3 days. How much is to be paid to C?

(a) 375

(b) 400

(c) 600

A, B & C can do a piece of work in 15, 20 & 30 days, respectively. If they work on alternate days, in how many days will the work be completed?

(a) 19

(b) 19 2/3

(c) 20

(d) 22

content:
## 0 reply

## Please log in or register to add a comment.

This method can be used for solve the question quickly . As mention in the question there are two men Atual and Anshu. And work to done by individual or together should be same. So let us assume Anshu time taken is x days so Atual time taken will be (2\ 4x/5)=8x/5

Now Solution

Men         Atual                           Anshu                      Atual+ Anshu

Rate        W/T = 1  (R1)           W/T = 8/5 (R2)          R1+R2 = 13/5

Time        8x/5                            x                                16(Given in Question)

Work       8x/5                            8x/5                               208/5 [...] GATE Overflow for GATE CSE
GATE Overflow for GATE CSE

# Time and Work

Atul does half as much work as Anshu in $\dfrac 4 5$ of the time. If together they take $16$ days to complete the work, how many days shall Anshu take to do it?

User Avatar

## 1 1 comment reply

User Avatar

## Please log in or register to add a comment.

## Please log in or register to answer this question.

## 4 Answers

User Avatar

## 0 reply

## Please log in or register to add a comment.

-->  Let Anshu takes x Days  ,  than Atul takes (2\(4/5)x) = 8x/5 Days.

--> Together Atul and Anshu takes = 16 Days . So, Together 1 Day work = 1 / 16 .

--> Taking 1(one) Day work of , Anshu + Atul = Together .

--> (1 / x) + (5 / 8x) = 1 / 16  , Solving x = (16\13) / 8 = 26 Days [Anshu].

User Avatar

## 0 reply [...] User Avatar

## 0 reply

## Please log in or register to add a comment.

$\text{Total work} =\text{Efficiency (or) One day work}\times \text{Total time}$

$\textbf{W = DMTE}$

D = Number of days

M = Number of men

T = Number of hours per day

E = Efficiency

W = Amount of work

Now, $D\_{1}M\_{1} = D\_{2}M\_{2}$

$\implies \dfrac{\frac{4}{5}\times M\_{1}}{\frac{1}{2} W} = \dfrac{1 \times M\_{2}}{W}$

$\implies 8M\_{1} = 5 M\_{2}$

$\implies M\_{1} = \dfrac{5}{8}M\_{2}$

again, $x \times M\_{2} =  16 \times (M\_{1} + M\_{2})$

$\implies x \times M\_{2} =  16 \times \bigg(\dfrac{5}{8}M\_{2} + M\_{2}\bigg)$

$\implies x \times M\_{2} = 16 \times \dfrac{13}{8}M\_{2}$

$\therefore x = 26$ days
